githubActivity.name: ReceiptPulse
githubActivity.note: Serverless receipt processing system using AWS Textract and Lambda.

projects.title: ReceiptPulse
projects.summary: I built ReceiptPulse as an AWS receipt workspace where each signed-in user can upload receipts, extract structured fields, resolve duplicates, and manage results. Designed to reduce manual expense tracking time by 80%.
projects.proof: This project proves event-driven AWS architecture, private user-scoped product flow, extraction workflows, review tooling, and product-grade dashboard delivery.
details: Built a full upload-to-insight pipeline around private per-user receipt processing.
Added sign-up/sign-in, receipt-only validation, duplicate decision handling, and analytics.
Pushed it beyond a backend demo by building a real dashboard flow around uploads, history, and stored results.
architecture: Signed-in users get a private workspace through Cognito-backed auth.
Receipt uploads land in user-scoped S3 paths.
Lambda processes files, validates them, and calls Textract.
The data is normalized, categorized, labeled, and stored in DynamoDB.
The dashboard code is versioned with the repo so the product flow remains inspectable.
projects.tradeoff: I optimized for a believable product-style AWS build with strong user flow first; deeper reporting and a portable redeploy path are the next layer.

projects.title: LumenStack AI
projects.summary: I built LumenStack AI as a codebase architecture analyzer that parses repositories and generates Mermaid diagrams. Architected to accelerate developer onboarding and technical reviews.
projects.proof: This project proves backend analysis flow, LLM orchestration, anti-hallucination guardrails, and multi-page product design.
details: Accepts repos or uploads and turns them into structured architecture summaries.
Added diagram generation, compare mode, retrieval-backed chat, and exports.
Designed to make unfamiliar codebases easier to understand instantly.
architecture: Repository input is parsed file by file.
Manifest and import patterns are used to infer dependency structure deterministically.
A structured analysis layer feeds diagrams and AI explanations.
The frontend presents the output as a guided, interactive developer product.
projects.tradeoff: Kept static parsing and AI explanation as separate parts so generated output is easier to review and explain.

learningLog.title: Operating a static-safe portfolio OS
learningLog.note: I am actively maintaining committed project snapshots, manifest branding, and project storytelling so live proof stays accurate without unsafe browser-side API calls.

learningLog.title: Making systems feel product-grade
learningLog.note: I am tightening AWS, AI, and frontend projects so each one feels like a production-style product, not a disconnected demo.

learningLog.title: Using motion with stronger visual hierarchy
learningLog.note: I am learning how motion, depth, and contrast can improve comprehension while still keeping technical clarity.

learningLog.title: Keeping repos and portfolio aligned
learningLog.note: I am using committed metadata snapshots so future project changes reflect in the portfolio with less manual rework.
